notes: Round-3 labeling.py ablation #6: same Platt consumption as sub33_label_platt, paired with a K-MEANS DIVERSITY acquisition_function (worked-example §3.6 design). Offline KMeans(n_clusters=64, random_state=0) fit on PCA-256 normalized training-item embeddings at module load; module-level Counter _seen tracks per-cluster visits within the round; score = -_seen[nearest_centroid] so first-of-its-cluster wins. K=5 picks per category then span 5 distinct semantic neighborhoods (assuming the pool covers them). This is the third row of the acquisition axis (random / uncertainty / OOD-diversity-centroid / kmeans-diversity-within-round).
